mappings.rationaleAI: Hybridization directly addresses interspecific reproduction mechanisms in plants (polyploidy) and animals (mules, ligers), barriers to reproduction (prezygotic and postzygotic isolation), and the role of reproductive processes in speciation and genetic diversity.

Behavioural ecology covers sexual selection and mate choice, parental investment, kin selection, and the evolution of reproductive strategies -- directly relevant to natural selection's shaping of reproduction and hormone cycles related to reproductive behaviour.

Slime moulds exhibit remarkable reproductive biology including spore formation, aggregation cycles, and life cycles that span sexual and asexual phases, relevant to cell reproduction, organism life cycles, and variations in reproductive cycles.

Fungi reproduce via spore production through both sexual and asexual means, with diverse reproductive strategies relevant to asexual systems creating identical organisms and sex organs in spore plants.
Pheromones play a central role in sexual attraction and reproductive signaling across animal species, directly relevant to mechanisms aiding gamete union and the role of gonads in hormone cycles.
Linnaeus developed the sexual system of plant classification based on reproductive structures (stamens and pistils), directly relevant to variations of sex organs in seed plants.
Circadian rhythms govern hormone release cycles including reproductive hormones, directly relevant to internal factors influencing maturation and the role of gonads in hormone cycles.
